skill.name: clerk-auth
skill.description: Guides Clerk authentication setup for Next.js App Router, including providers, sign-in components, route protection, and server auth state.
skill.summary: Implement Clerk authentication patterns for Next.js App Router projects.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported two high-severity weak cryptographic algorithm matches at SKILL.md lines 3 and 15. Manual review found both matches are false positives in documentation text for Clerk authentication setup. No executable code, prompt injection, data exfiltration, network activity, filesystem access, or command execution evidence was found.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Finding Dismissed
low_findings.description: The scanner flagged SKILL.md lines 3 and 15 as weak cryptographic algorithm usage. Line 3 is a skill description about authentication patterns, and line 15 references environment variables in setup documentation. Neither line contains a cryptographic algorithm, executable code, secret handling implementation, or unsafe crypto routine.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are plain Markdown documentation and contain no hash, cipher, random number generation, or cryptographic API usage. The file has no scripts or executable implementation that could create a weak-crypto risk.

content.value_statement: Authentication setup can be error-prone when providers, middleware, and server state are split across files. This skill guides Clerk setup for Next.js App Router with focused patterns for protected routes and user access.
actual_capabilities: Explains ClerkProvider placement for Next.js App Router projects.
Guides use of Clerk sign-in, sign-up, and user menu components.
Describes route protection with clerkMiddleware and createRouteMatcher.
Recommends centralizing authentication logic in a root middleware file.
Shows when to use auth() and currentUser() in Server Components.
limitations: Does not provide complete webhook implementation guidance.
Does not include detailed organization management workflows.
Does not validate Clerk dashboard settings or production keys.
Does not replace review of current Clerk and Next.js documentation.

use_cases.title: Add Authentication to a New App
use_cases.description: Set up Clerk providers and user interface components in a Next.js App Router project.

use_cases.title: Protect Application Routes
use_cases.description: Plan middleware rules for public pages, private dashboards, and authenticated route groups.

use_cases.title: Read User State on the Server
use_cases.description: Use server authentication helpers to access user, session, and organization identifiers.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Clerk Setup
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a new Next.js App Router project.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me add Clerk authentication to my Next.js App Router project. Include provider placement, sign-in pages, sign-up pages, and required environment variables.

prompt_templates.title: Protect Route Groups
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my Next.js route structure and propose Clerk middleware protection rules. Separate public pages from authenticated pages and explain each rule.

prompt_templates.title: Use Server Auth State
prompt_templates.prompt: Show how to access Clerk user and session state in Server Components. Explain when to use auth() and when to use currentUser().

prompt_templates.title: Audit Clerk Integration
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review of an existing implementation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit my Clerk authentication setup for missing middleware, misplaced providers, unsafe route access, and server-side user state mistakes.

output_examples.input: Add Clerk to a new Next.js App Router project.
output_examples.output: A setup plan covering provider placement, environment variable names, sign-in and sign-up routes, and user menu placement.

output_examples.input: Protect my dashboard routes but keep marketing pages public.
output_examples.output: A route protection plan that identifies public routes, private route groups, and middleware checks for authenticated access.

output_examples.input: Explain how server components should read Clerk auth state.
output_examples.output: A comparison of lightweight session access and full user object access, with guidance on middleware requirements.

best_practices: Keep authentication logic centralized in one root middleware file.
Use route matchers to make public and protected paths explicit.
Read only the user data needed for each server-side request.
anti_patterns: Do not scatter independent authentication checks across unrelated route files.
Do not assume Server Component auth helpers work without Clerk middleware.
Do not expose secret keys or production credentials in client-side code.

faq.question: What framework does this skill focus on?
faq.answer: It focuses on Clerk authentication patterns for Next.js 14 and 15 App Router projects.

faq.question: Can it help with middleware route protection?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers clerkMiddleware, createRouteMatcher, and explicit route protection with auth.protect().

faq.question: Does it include prebuilt sign-in components?
faq.answer: It references Clerk SignIn, SignUp, and UserButton components as standard building blocks.

faq.question: Does it cover Server Components?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains use of auth() and currentUser() for server-side authentication state.

faq.question: Does it implement webhooks?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed skill file does not provide a full webhook implementation.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The static findings were reviewed as false positives, and no dangerous behavior was found.
